data.title: The Fed should continue slow balance sheet runoff, Hammack says
data.description:
The Federal Reserve should keep shrinking its balance sheet, albeit slowly, through a period of fiscal uncertainty, Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack said in a recent interview with Reuters.

Her "baseline" preference is for the Fed continuing the central bank's balance sheet runoff, known as quantitative tightening (or QT), as the government hashes out spending plans and adjusts the debt limit to meet its borrowing needs, she said, according to a Reuters report published on Friday.

Minutes from the Federal Open Market Committee's January meeting showed that some officials suggested slowing or pausing the QT process to deal with uncertainty over the government's finances.

Shrinking the balance sheet reduces reserves across the U.S. banking system. Reducing liquidity too much or too fast during a period of uncertainty can lead to money market turmoil, and upset the Fed's control over short-term rates, its main tool to influence the economy.

Repo operations can help the Fed manage the reduced liquidity the Treasury cash management will create as it manages its finance after a debt ceiling resolution, Hammack said. Regarding QT, "I do think it can go a bit longer," she added.

Once the fiscal plans are in place, the Fed can employ temporary bond purchases, or repos, if needed, "to put more (liquidity) back into the system until you figure out" the market's needs, Hammack said.

Once government funding issues are resolved, while there may be adequate reserves, "if the distribution of it is not as efficient across the system, you need some mechanism for getting it to all the right places," she said.

In a speech on Thursday, Hammack urged a patient approach to adjusting the federal funds rate further. With the economy staying stable, the Fed has "the luxury of being patient as we assess the path forward," she said.

During the Reuters interview, she said she doesn't expect a rate hike if inflation remains above the Fed's 2% target, but the odds of a rate increase are not zero.

Meanwhile, it's too soon to say what the Trump administration's tariff plans mean for inflation, she said. It will depend on what is implemented and how other countries respond.

data.title: Dell dips after mixed Q4, but analysts remain positive
data.description:
Shares of Dell Technologies (NYSE:DELL) fell about 6% premarket on Friday after fiscal 2025 fourth quarter revenue missed estimates, and first quarter revenue outlook came in lighter, however, Wall Street analysts maintained bullish views on the stock.

BofA Securities reiterated its Buy rating and $150 price target on the shares.

Analysts led by Wamsi Mohan said Dell reported around in-line fiscal fourth 2025 earnings results. At a high-level, fiscal first quarter guidance was lighter than expected but full fiscal year 2026 outlook was about in-line with BofA's estimates — which the analysts believe will prove conservative.

AI demand remains strong, and Dell noted the backlog intra-quarter has grown to $9B ($5B xAI deal). Despite the increase in demand, Dell has been able to maintain operating margins due to storage growth (higher margin) that should keep improving as Dell pivotsto its own IP, the analysts added.

On Client Solutions Group, or CSG, the analysts said they remain cautious due to commodity pressure and potential tariffs. They believe Dell will be more resilient versus peers because of their high mix in commercial, and will raise prices to offset any tariff effects.

"Overall, Dell is set up well to accelerate growth in F26 and we expect their guide to be revised upwards as we move through F26. Reiterate Buy as we are still in the early stages of AI adoption, margins from mix, and tailwind from upcoming PC refresh and longer-term AI PC adoption," said Mohan and his team.

Morgan Stanley kept its Overweight rating and $128 price target on Dell's stock.

Analysts led by Erik W Woodring said Dell guided to a stronger-than-expected fiscal 2026 EPS, talked about accelerating momentum in AI servers, and is benefiting from AI adoption and scale to maintain operating margins despite gross margin pressure.

The analysts noted that while their fiscal 2026 EPS remains around $9.50, they believe a pathto more than $10 of EPS is clearer post-earnings.

Meanwhile, Mizuho reiterated its Outperform rating on Dell but lowered the price target to $140 from $150.

The analysts said Dell reported January quarter (fiscal fourth quarter) revenue of $23.9B (below consensus $24.6B), while guiding April quarter (fiscal first) down about 4% quarter-over-quarter to $23.0B (slightly below consensus of around $23.6B) and fiscal 2026 up nearly 8% year-over-year to about $103B (in line with consensus $103B) with Al server strength, Storage/conventionalserver growing, and second half of calendar year 2025 PC refresh.

Related stocks: HP Inc (HPQ), which also reported its quarterly results, saw its stock dip about 3% premarket on Friday.

data.title: AbbVie gets EU nod for its giant cell arteritis treatment
data.description:
AbbVie (NYSE:ABBV) announced Friday that an expert panel of the EU drug regulator, the European Medicines Agency (EMA), endorsed Upadacitinib as a treatment for giant cell arteritis.

Issuing a positive opinion, EMA’s Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use (CHMP) has recommended the marketing authorization for upadacitinib or RINVOQ 15 mg for the treatment of adult patients with giant cell arteritis, an autoimmune disease leading to inflammation of the blood vessels.

The CHMP recommendation will next be reviewed by the European Commission (EC) before a final decision on marketing authorization, which is expected in the first half of 2025.

data.title: Nayax acquires UPPay
data.description:
• Nayax (NASDAQ:NYAX) on Friday announced the acquisition of UPPay, a digital payment and telemetry provider for automated self-service coffee machines in Brazil.
  • The purchase price, equating to an enterprise value of R$ 32.75 million BRL (~$5.3 million), will be paid with cash on hand.
  • The company said the strategic acquisition will significantly increase Nayax’s Brazilian footprint and unlock new market opportunities across Latin America with a sophisticated suite of affordable solutions.
  • The company said UPPay will integrate operationally with VMtecnologia, a technology provider for the automated self-service industry in Brazil, consolidating both companies' sales, distributional, and technical expertise to offer a wider combined range of solutions, target new verticals like coffee vending, and improve margins with synergies.
  • NYAX -1.11% premarket to $37.24.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Eli Lilly in pact with Magnet Bio to develop cancer drugs in a deal worth up to $1.25B
data.description:
Magnet Biomedicine announced Friday it has reached a licensing agreement worth up to $1.25B with Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) to develop cancer drugs leveraging the company’s TrueGlue discovery platform.

As part of the deal, Boston, the Massachusetts-based firm will receive up to $40M in the form of upfront and near-term payments as well as equity from LLY as the duo initiates studies to develop glue therapeutics for cancer using Magnet’s TrueGlue platform.

The company is also eligible to receive tiered royalties on global net sales and more than $1.25B worth of milestone payments subject to the achievement of development, regulatory, and commercial milestones.

Magnet’s TrueGlue platform is designed to develop small molecules that can promote protein-to-protein interactions and generate clinically meaningful therapeutic responses in patients.

"Lilly's commitment to innovation and deep expertise in bringing transformative medicines to patients makes it an ideal partner for Magnet as we harness the untapped potential of molecular glues,” Magnet’s CEO Brian Safina said.

data.title: Ollie's accelerates pace of store openings as it takes advantage of the Big Lots bankruptcy
data.description:
Ollie's Bargain Outlet Holdings (NASDAQ:OLLI) announced the acquisition of 40 former Big Lots store leases from Gordon Brothers. The acquisition of the additional store leases is subject to final bankruptcy court approval and customary closing conditions.

The retailer has been active in picking through the scraps of the Big Lots bankruptcy, and has now acquired a total of 63 former Big Lots store leases to date.

"Everything about these stores lines up well with our business and growth strategy. These locations are the right size, come with favorable lease terms, are located in existing and adjacent trade areas, and have long serviced value conscious consumers," highlighted Ollie's (NASDAQ:OLLI) CEO Eric van der Valk.

Ollie's (OLLI) plans to adjust its existing new store openings and prioritize the opening of the acquired stores in a manner that makes the most operational and financial sense. The acquisition of the Big Lots store leases are expected to provide the company with the opportunity to accelerate new store openings in 2025 above the stated 10% annual growth target and open approximately 75 units.

Ollie's (OLLI) currently operates 568 stores in 31 states.

Share of Ollie's (OLLI) were up 1.6% in premarket trading.
